title: Dinebra somalensis
taxonomy: Dinebra somalensis is a species of grass in the family Poaceae. It is native to Somalia and has been identified as a rare and unique species. Dinebra somalensis is classified as a monocot, which means it has one cotyledon or seed leaf in its seedling stage. Within the family Poaceae, Dinebra somalensis is classified in the subfamily Chloridoideae. This subfamily includes many species of grasses found in tropical and subtropical regions.

intro: Dinebra somalensis, also known as Somali grass, is a plant species that belongs to the family Poaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues, due to its reported health benefits of reducing inflammation and improving immune function. Bioactive constituents of Dinebra somalensis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant is used to make herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are used to treat various health conditions, although its efficacy and safety have not been extensively scientifically studied.
